fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alaska Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alaska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity alaska, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to alaska, respiratory therapist compact alaska, alaska respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Alaska DCBPL (No RT License Required)
board.parent_dept: Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing
board.mailing_address:
State of Alaska
Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing
PO Box 110806
Juneau, AK 99811-0806

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Alaska.

steps.title: Establish a MyAlaska Account
steps.description: Create a user account on the State of Alaska's MyAlaska portal (my.alaska.gov). This account is essential for accessing the MyLicense online services.

steps.title: Complete Online Application via MyLicense
steps.description: Log in to MyAlaska, navigate to the MyLicense online services (commerce.alaska.gov/cbpl/mylicense/), and complete the 'Respiratory Care Practitioner License' application. Ensure you select the appropriate application type, such as 'Endorsement' if you are licensed in another state.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts (if applicable), NBRC credentials, and verification of any out-of-state licenses to be sent directly to the Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing. Specific instructions for document submission are provided within the MyLicense application.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $100 through the MyLicense system.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: The Division will review your application and supporting documents. Monitor your application status through the MyLicense portal and respond promptly to any requests for additional information.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Alaska respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Alaska have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Alaska. To work in Alaska, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Alaska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alaska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Alaska
hero.dek: To apply for an Alaska Respiratory Care Practitioner license by endorsement, you must create a MyAlaska account and complete the application through the MyLicense online services portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit my.alaska.gov to create a MyAlaska account. This account is required to access the MyLicense online services where the application is submitted.
